title: Make iw item-status the runtime source of truth for step list and per-step runtime info

steps.description: Add nullable command/gate/timeout_secs columns to workflow_steps via Alembic; update WorkflowStep model with matching mapped columns
Review S01 schema migration + model update for correctness, reversibility, and naming conventions

Update iw register to populate command/gate/timeout_secs from manifest at ingest; rewrite manifest in place adding _note field; add fallback wiring in daemon read paths (batch_manager._build_claude_prompt, batch_manager._compute_qv_baselines, fix_cycle._get_gate_name_and_command) so legacy NULL rows still work via manifest read
Review S03 register-side ingest, manifest stamping, and daemon fallback wiring
Enrich iw item-status --json per-step output with prompt_file, command, gate, timeout_secs, agent_label, opencode_agent (true superset of the manifest)
Review S05 JSON enrichment for shape, key naming, and null handling

Add a one-line 'prefer iw item-status' hint to 8 prompt templates (Implementation/CodeReview/CodeReview_Final/QualityValidation in templates/design/ and the matching ai-dev/templates/ mirrors). Do NOT modify QualityValidation_FIX templates.
Review S07 template edits for consistency across the 8 files and that QualityValidation_FIX templates were not touched

Unit tests for register-side ingest + manifest stamping; unit tests for item-status JSON shape; integration round-trip test (register → item-status); regression test that daemon fallbacks work for legacy NULL rows; explicit AC6 regression test (I-00041 S14/S15 scenario)
Review S09 tests for semantic correctness and coverage of the 6 acceptance criteria
Cross-step global review of S01/S03/S05/S07/S09 — verify schema → register → CLI → templates → tests chain holds end-to-end and the daemon fallback contract is correct
